I agree with the initial assessment that option A is the correct choice. However, adding another point might strengthen our argument against option D being false. Supracondylar fractures often involve disruption of the ulnar collateral ligament complex due to their high-energy nature, leading to permanent weakness in elbow extension rather than just flexion.
